description: Fix ConfigDirectory illustration (wrong auth.json location and annotation) and contributing docs inaccuracies
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix ConfigDirectory SVG illustration: auth.json location and annotation
userStories.description: As a user reading the configuration docs, I want the directory tree illustration to show auth.json in the correct location so that I can find my config files
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigDirectory illustration in illustrations.tsx shows auth.json inside the extension/ subdirectory (at ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json), not as a top-level file alongside config.json
ConfigDirectory illustration annotation for auth.json says 'Auth secret' (not 'Auth secret + port') since auth.json contains only the secret, not the port
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two issues in docs/components/illustrations.tsx (the ConfigDirectory SVG component):

1. Lines 722-734: auth.json is shown as a top-level file inside ~/.opentabs/, alongside config.json, audit.log, and server.log. But auth.json actually lives at ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json. See platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts lines 370-375: writeAuthFile writes to join(getExtensionDir(), 'auth.json').

2. Line 733: The annotation reads 'Auth secret + port (written on server start)'. But auth.json contains only { "secret": "<hex>" } — no port. The port is resolved from --port flag or OPENTABS_PORT env var. Change annotation to 'Auth secret (written on server start)'.

This requires modifying SVG layout to show auth.json nested under an extension/ directory entry. The SVG uses absolute y-coordinates for positioning, so you'll need to add an 'extension/' directory entry and adjust the y-positions of subsequent items. Use opus model since SVG illustration editing requires careful spatial reasoning.

The text documentation in docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx correctly describes the path — only the illustration is wrong.

userStories.title: Fix dev-setup and publishing docs inaccuracies
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want accurate build and publishing documentation so that I understand the actual tooling behavior
acceptanceCriteria: dev-setup.mdx correctly states that npm run build uses `tsc --build` (incremental, not --force), and notes that `npm run build:force` is available for full clean rebuilds

publishing.mdx accurately describes what the publish script does: git status check, lockfile regeneration, build:force (not build), plugin dependency updates, changelog generation, and git commit/tag creation
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two issues:

1. docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx line 33: Says step 1 of the build pipeline is 'tsc --build --force'. The root package.json build script uses 'tsc --build' (without --force). The --force variant is 'npm run build:force'. Update to say 'tsc --build' and note that build:force exists for full clean rebuilds.

2. docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx lines 93-97: The publish script description omits several automated steps. The actual script (scripts/publish.ts) also: checks for uncommitted git changes, deletes package-lock.json and runs npm install, uses build:force (not build), updates plugin dependencies and rebuilds plugins, generates a changelog, creates a git commit and tag. Also line 96 says 'Runs npm run build again' but the script uses 'npm run build:force'. Update the step list to be comprehensive.
